inline_review:
commit 7c46736066ba247c6f8bd520a10a070dcd650d09
Author: David Carlier <devnexen@gmail.com>

mm/memcontrol: fix obj_cgroup leak in mem_cgroup_css_online() error path

This commit fixes an obj_cgroup leak in the mem_cgroup_css_online() error
path by adding a missing percpu_ref_kill() when obj_cgroup_alloc() fails.

> diff --git a/mm/memcontrol.c b/mm/memcontrol.c
> index 23c57d950011..0153efacd49e 100644
> --- a/mm/memcontrol.c
> +++ b/mm/memcontrol.c

[ ... ]

> @@ -4172,6 +4173,9 @@ static int mem_cgroup_css_online(struct cgroup_subsys_state *css)
>  free_objcg:
>  	for_each_node(nid) {
>  		struct mem_cgroup_per_node *pn = memcg->nodeinfo[nid];
> +		objcg = rcu_dereference_protected(pn->objcg, true);
> +		if (objcg)
> +			percpu_ref_kill(&objcg->refcnt);

Should pn->objcg be cleared before initiating the destruction of objcg?

According to RCU lifecycle rules, an object must be removed from all
RCU-protected data structures before its destruction is deferred to prevent
concurrent readers from observing a dangling pointer.

Because the memcg was already linked into the cgroup tree at the start of
onlining, can cgroup iterators (e.g., for_each_mem_cgroup()) access it and
create a use-after-free window?

Would it be safer to clear the pointer first using
rcu_replace_pointer(pn->objcg, NULL, true), matching the normal teardown
sequence in memcg_reparent_objcgs()?

>  		if (pn && pn->orig_objcg) {
>  			obj_cgroup_put(pn->orig_objcg);
> +			/*
> +			 * Reset pn->orig_objcg to NULL to prevent
> +			 * obj_cgroup_put() from being called again in
> +			 * __mem_cgroup_free().
> +			 */
> +			pn->orig_objcg = NULL;
>  		}
>  	}

The comment mentions preventing obj_cgroup_put() from being called again
in __mem_cgroup_free().

During cgroup creation, mem_cgroup_alloc() iterates over all nodes to
allocate per-node info. If alloc_mem_cgroup_per_node_info(memcg, node)
fails due to memory pressure, memcg->nodeinfo[node] remains NULL for the
failing node and jumps to fail:, invoking __mem_cgroup_free(memcg).

Does __mem_cgroup_free() unconditionally dereference pn when looping over
nodes and evaluating pn->orig_objcg?

If pn is NULL for the nodes that failed allocation, could evaluating
pn->orig_objcg trigger a NULL pointer dereference? Is a simple if (pn)
check required before accessing its fields in __mem_cgroup_free()?
output:
{"findings":[{"problem":"The error path fails to clear the RCU-protected pointer `pn->objcg` before initiating the destruction of the `objcg` object, violating RCU lifecycle rules.","severity":"High","severity_explanation":"According to RCU subsystem rules, an object must be removed from all RCU-protected data structures BEFORE its destruction is deferred, to prevent concurrent readers from observing a dangling pointer. In the patched `free_objcg` error path, `percpu_ref_kill(&objcg->refcnt)` and `obj_cgroup_put(pn->orig_objcg)` are called, which drop the refcount to zero and schedule `obj_cgroup_release()` (which ultimately calls `kfree_rcu()`). However, the RCU-protected pointer `pn->objcg` is never cleared. Because the `memcg` was already linked into the cgroup tree at the start of onlining, cgroup iterators (e.g., `for_each_mem_cgroup()`) can access it, creating a use-after-free window. The pointer should be cleared first (e.g., using `rcu_replace_pointer(pn->objcg, NULL, true)`), matching the normal teardown sequence in `memcg_reparent_objcgs()`."},{"problem":"Unconditional dereference of `pn` in `__mem_cgroup_free()` causes a guaranteed kernel panic if `alloc_mem_cgroup_per_node_info()` fails.","severity":"Critical","severity_explanation":"During cgroup creation, `mem_cgroup_alloc()` iterates over all nodes to allocate per-node info. If `alloc_mem_cgroup_per_node_info(memcg, node)` fails due to memory pressure, `memcg->nodeinfo[node]` remains NULL for the failing node and the function jumps to the `fail:` label, invoking `__mem_cgroup_free(memcg)`. Inside `__mem_cgroup_free()`, the code loops over all nodes and unconditionally evaluates `pn->orig_objcg` to pass to `obj_cgroup_put()`:
```c
		struct mem_cgroup_per_node *pn = memcg->nodeinfo[node];
		obj_cgroup_put(pn->orig_objcg); // CRASH
```
Because `pn` is NULL for the nodes that failed allocation, evaluating `pn->orig_objcg` attempts to read memory at address `0 + offsetof(orig_objcg)`, triggering a guaranteed NULL pointer dereference and kernel panic. This represents a trivial local DoS vulnerability under memory exhaustion. A simple `if (pn)` check is strictly required before accessing its fields."}],"fixes":"","review_inline":"commit 7c46736066ba247c6f8bd520a10a070dcd650d09
